title: Tangy Chewy Syrian Green Pepper Flatbread
description: A hearty, chewy flatbread infused with fresh green pepper, sumac, and a zesty lemon-cumin blend, perfect for latenight cravings.
instructions: In a small bowl, dissolve yeast (and optional sugar) in warm water; let sit 5 minutes until foamy.
In a large mixing bowl, whisk together whole wheat flour and salt.
Add the yeast mixture, olive oil, lemon zest, lemon juice, sumac, cumin, and paprika to the dry ingredients.
Stir until a shaggy dough forms, then knead on a floured surface for about 8 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover, and let rise in a warm spot for 30 minutes, until doubled in size.
Turn the dough onto a lightly floured surface and divide into 8 equal portions.
Roll each portion into a disc about 8 inches in diameter; place on a parchment‑lined baking sheet.
Top each flatbread with an even layer of finely chopped green pepper, pressing lightly to settle.
Bake at 425°F (220°C) for 12–15 minutes, or until the edges are golden and the center is lightly set.
Remove from oven, allow to cool 5 minutes, then sprinkle with chopped parsley before serving.
